clues.clue: This flat, segmented milk-chocolate bar comes from a Pennsylvania town famous for cocoa.
'Gimme a break' pitches this crispy four-finger wafer bar.
This caramel-nougat-and-peanut bar was named after the candy maker's favorite horse.
This bar splits into two chocolate-coated caramel-and-cookie sticks.
This Mars bar of nougat and caramel claims it 'really satisfies.'
'Melts in your mouth, not in your hands' is the long-time line for these candy-shelled chocolates.
Among the M&M's spokescandies, this nervous-voiced color often plays the dim one.
'Sometimes you feel like a nut' pairs Almond Joy with this nut-free coconut cousin.
Skittles invites fans to 'taste the' this colorful arc.
PEZ candy is dispensed from collectible toppers shaped like these cartoon noggins.
These translucent, chewy bear-shaped candies come in fruit flavors.
These long, twisted red licorice-style ropes are a Hershey's chewy treat.
These individually wrapped, chewy fruit squares are sold in a pink-heavy pack.
This pulled, pastel boardwalk treat is named for the sea despite containing little of it.
Sour gummy worms get their pucker from a coating of this tangy crystalline acid.
Reese's Cups are filled with chocolate and this creamy nut spread.
A foil-wrapped chocolate teardrop called a 'Kiss' is made by this brand.
Whoppers and Maltesers share this airy, toasted-grain center.
Each Ferrero Rocher hides a whole one of these round tree nuts in its center.
A Cadbury Creme Egg is filled with fondant dyed to mimic this golden part of a real egg.
Milton Hershey built an entire company town for his chocolate works in this state.
Besides candy, the Mars company is a giant maker of this product for cats and dogs.
PEZ began in this European country as a compressed peppermint breath candy.
Candy corn's three traditional bands are white, orange and this.
The chocolate maker Cadbury was founded in this country, also home to its Dairy Milk bar.

final.clue: This brand's chocolate-and-peanut-butter cups regularly top surveys of favorite trick-or-treat candy.
